Thank you for your inquiry about the **PM-CAR1000**, also known as the **Mock CD28 CAR-T**. Here are the details:

- **Catalog Number**: PM-CAR1000  
- **Name**: Mock CD28 CAR-T  
- **Target Antigen**: Mock  
- **Formulation**: CS10 CryoStor Medium (Sigma Aldrich, Cat. #C2874)  
- **Shipping**: Dry ice at -70°C  
- **Storage**: Liquid nitrogen  
- **Construct**: mock-TM28-CD28-CD3ζ  
- **Costimulatory Domain**: CD28  
- **Price**: $1259 USD  
- **Lead Time**: (not on file)

      • price: `1259`

      • formulation: `CS10 CryoStor Medium (Sigma Aldrich, Cat. #C2874)`
      • shipping: `Dry ice at -70°C`
      • storage: `Liquid nitrogen`
      • description: `CD19 is a type I membrane protein expressed by all B cells and B cell precursors but not by plasma cells or other cell types. CD19 functions as part of the B cell antigen receptor complex, allowing B cells to respond to foreign antigens. CD19 is also expressed on all B cell malignancies and is a favorable target for immunotherapy because of its specificity; patients receive immunoglobulin injections because their own B cells are depleted along with the malignant cells. 
 
 
ProMab's PM-CAR1042 primary human CAR-T cells express an anti-CD19 CAR containing a humanized CD19-specific scFv, hinge and transmembrane domains, the CD28 costimulatory domain and the CD3zeta activation domain. The CAR-T cells are generated by transducing activated human T cells with replication-defective lentivirus encoding the CAR. After expansion, the cells are cryopreserved and shipped in vials containing up to 10 million cells. Please inquire for orders over 10 million cells. All ProMab cells are guaranteed to be sterile, pathogen-free and mycoplasma-free.`
      • costimulatory_domain: `CD28`
      • group_name: `CAR-T Cells`
      • group_type: `cell_product`

The PM-CAR1009, also known as CD19 CD28 CAR-T, is used for targeting the CD19 antigen. It is part of the CAR-T/CAR-NK business line and is designed for applications in immunotherapy, particularly in the treatment of certain types of blood cancers such as B-cell malignancies. Thank you for your inquiry about the cell number for catalog product PM-CAR1042. 

The product is the Humanized CD19 CAR-T cells, and it is shipped in vials containing up to 10 million cells. If you need to order more than 10 million cells, please let me know, and I can assist you further.

If you have any other questions or need additional information, feel free to ask!

      • catalog #: `PM-CAR1042`

      • formulation: `CS10 CryoStor Medium (Sigma Aldrich, Cat. #C2874)`
      • shipping: `Dry ice at -70°C`
      • storage: `Liquid nitrogen`
      • description: `CD19 is a type I membrane protein expressed by all B cells and B cell precursors but not by plasma cells or other cell types. CD19 functions as part of the B cell antigen receptor complex, allowing B cells to respond to foreign antigens. CD19 is also expressed on all B cell malignancies and is a favorable target for immunotherapy because of its specificity; patients receive immunoglobulin injections because their own B cells are depleted along with the malignant cells. 
 
 
ProMab's PM-CAR1042 primary human CAR-T cells express an anti-CD19 CAR containing a humanized CD19-specific scFv, hinge and transmembrane domains, the CD28 costimulatory domain and the CD3zeta activation domain. The CAR-T cells are generated by transducing activated human T cells with replication-defective lentivirus encoding the CAR. After expansion, the cells are cryopreserved and shipped in vials containing up to 10 million cells. Please inquire for orders over 10 million cells. All ProMab cells are guaranteed to be sterile, pathogen-free and mycoplasma-free.`
      • costimulatory_domain: `CD28`
      • group_name: `CAR-T Cells`
      • group_type: `cell_product`

[ProMab LNP Formulation | Exhausted T-cells] Customer message:
I am interested in LNP formulations to deliver mRNA cargo to non-activated/exhausted T cells. Other companies generate LDLR-dependent LNPs and preclude their use for non-activated T cells. Please let me know if you produce any LNP formulations that can deliver cargo to non-activated/exhausted T cells. Thank you.

---

Sales reply:
Dear Brandon Simone,

Thank you for your inquiry about ProMab's mRNA-LNP technology service. We offer a comprehensive platform for custom mRNA, custom lipid nanoparticle formulation, as well as scale-up and validation services.

Our website product list has a bunch of ready-to-use products that have been tested in T cells before. The formulation for each of them is standard but we have used multiple commercial formulations as well as our in-house formulations. However, I presume most of them have been tested in activated T cells. I do not know their efficacy in non-activated/exhausted T cells, but we are happy to perform this assay for you if you are interested in custom services. Alternatively, you can purchase the LNPs and test them in your assay.

If you would like to develop a custom formulation, we'll be happy to perform either way. I am sure there is some way we can work together. Please let me know if you have any questions and thank you again for your inquiry today. *💰 Live catalog / pricing facts* _(from postgres — authoritative)_

   _Catalog lookup matches:_

   *[1]* CD70 CAR mRNA-LNP
      • catalog #: `PM-LNP-0050`
      • price: `1038`
      • currency: `USD`
      • lead time: `(not on file)`
      • business line: `mRNA-LNP`
      • record type: `CAR`
      • format: `10ug in 200ul`
      • also known as: `PM-LNP-0050, CD70 CAR mRNA-LNP, CAR, 10ug in 200ul, CD70 CAR mRNA LNP, CD70 CAR mRNA Lipid Nanoparticle, CD70 CAR LNP mRNA`
      • formulation: `mRNA-LNPs suspended in PBS (-Ca, -Mg) (pH: 7.0-7.4).`
      • shipping: `Product is delivered on blue ice. Store at 4°C for up to 3 months.`
      • storage: `4°C; ice`
      • description: `CD70 (Cluster of Differentiation 70), encoded by the CD70 gene, is a cytokine belonging to the tumor necrosis factor (TNF) ligand family. This cytokine is the surface antigen and ligand of TNFRSF27/CD27. It is primarily expressed on highly activated T and B cells, as well as NK cells and mature dendritic cells. CD70 expression on T and B cells is stimulated by triggering T and B cell receptors and can be upregulated by several cytokines such as IL-1Î±, IL-2, IL-12, GM-CSF and TNF-Î±. As a co-stimulatory molecule, CD70 plays an important role in regulating immune system activation, in particular by improving the activation, proliferation and survival of T and B cells, resulting in a more effective immune response. CD70 has been shown to be overexpressed in several types of cancer, including Hodgkin's and non-Hodgkin's lymphomas, as well as certain types of solid tumors. Overexpression of CD70 in cancer cells correlates with cell proliferation and survival, and inhibits apoptosis, in cancer progression. Therefore, CD70 is considered as a possible target for the treatment of CD70-positive lymphomas. This product is designed as a tool for the delivery and expression of anti-CD70 CAR mRNA for research. The product leverages the lipid nanoparticle (LNP) technology platform for simple and efficient delivery of anti-CD70 CAR mRNA to a variety of mammalian cells in vitro and in vivo. The LNPs used are formulated with SM-102, DSPC, cholesterol and DMG-PEG2000 at an optimal molar concentration for a high rate of encapsulation and efficient mRNA delivery. The anti-CD70 CAR in this product is approximately 56 kD and consists of anti-CD70 scFv (single-chain variable fragment) linked to a second-generation CAR (chimeric antigen receptor) containing the CD8 hinge and transmembrane domain and 4-1BB and CD3Î¶ signaling domains. The full-length amino acid sequence of this anti-CD70 CAR mRNA-LNP product is available upon request.`
      • lnp_type: `CAR`
      • lnp_application: `Chimeric antigen receptor`
      • application_handling: `Upon receiving product, briefly pulse spin before opening to ensure product is at bottom of container. It is important not to spin for too long as this may rupture mRNA-LNPs. Do not vortex. Work with mRNA-LNPs on ice and minimize the time that the product spends at room temperature. After handling the product during experiments, return immediately to ice. mRNA-LNP products should only be handled with certified RNase-free reagents and consumables. Use of filtered pipette tips is highly recommended.`
      • cell_type_tested: `HEK293S`
      • data_sheet_url: `https://files.promab.com/strapi/PM_LNP_0050_3d56672257.pdf`

Sales reply:
Dear Kangfu Chen,

Thank you for your inquiry about ProMab's mRNA-LNP products! I am happy to hear that you are interested in our platform. Our LNPs are formulated with SM-102, DSPC, cholesterol, and DMG-PEG2000, and the size is under 100nm.

Please let me know if you have any further questions or would like to proceed with a quote. Thank you again Kangfu for your inquiry and I look forward to your reply.

[ProMab mCherry mRNA-LNP PM-LNP-0022 (200ul)] Customer message:
What's mRNA mass encapsulated in PM-LNP-0022 (200 ul)?

---

Sales reply:
Hello Jiulong Yu,

Thank you for your inquiry about ProMab's lipid nanoparticle products!

We encapsulate 10ug in 200ul of mRNA-LNP in all of our catalog products.

We can do custom production or different concentrations of RNA as a custom service if desired. Here is a link to the flyer for this platform: mRNA-LNP Products & Services.pdf: https://hubs.ly/Q01R-4F10

Let me know if you have any other questions about our mRNA-LNP products/services and I would be happy to help.

---

Sales reply:
Hi Jonathan,

Sure thing! Here is the full AA of the CAR:
*

The CAR structure is as follows:
CD19scFv-Hing/TM-41BB-CD3z
queries.final_message:
*📝 Draft reply* _(CSR: please review & edit before sending)_

Dear Customer,

The encoding details for the product PM-LNP-0010, which is the COVID-19 Spike Protein (Alpha Variant) mRNA-LNP, are as follows:

- **Formulation**: mRNA-LNPs suspended in PBS (-Ca, -Mg) (pH: 7.0-7.4).
- **LNP Composition**: The lipid nanoparticles (LNPs) are formulated with SM-102, DSPC, cholesterol, and DMG-PEG2000 at optimal molar concentrations for high encapsulation efficiency and effective mRNA delivery.

For more detailed information, you can refer to the [data sheet here](https://files.promab.com/strapi/PM_LNP_0010_c84637e325.pdf).

If you have any further questions or need additional information, feel free to ask!

Best regards,

ProMab Biotechnologies Customer Service

The best-known vaccines we use are the spike protein vaccines delivered via mRNA. While the role of vaccination in controlling outbreaks is undeniable, the recurring emergence of new variants of Covid-19, such as the Delta and Omega variants, poses new challenges, particularly their rapid global spread.
This product is designed as a tool for the delivery and expression of full-length spike protein mRNA of COVID-19 Strain Alpha for vaccine research.
This product utilizes the lipid nanoparticle (LNP) technology platform for simple and efficient delivery of the Alpha variant spike protein (COVID-19) mRNA to a variety of mammalian cells in vitro and in vivo.  The LNP’s used are formulated with, SM-102, DSPC, cholesterol, and DMG-PEG2000 at optimal molar concentration for a high rate of encapsulation and efficient mRNA delivery. Alpha Spike protein consists of 1273 amino acids and its GenPept accession number is YP_009724390.`

      • application_handling: `Upon receiving product, briefly pulse spin before opening to ensure product is at bottom of container. After handling the product during experiments, return immediately to ice. mRNA-LNP products should only be handled with certified RNase-free reagents and consumables. Use of filtered pipette tips is highly recommended. `

ProMab Biotechnologies Customer Service

queries.final_message:
*📝 Draft reply* _(CSR: please review & edit before sending)_ Thank you for your inquiry about catalog product 10007. Here are the details: - **Name:** STAT3 Primary Antibody - **Catalog Number:** 10007 - **Type:** Rabbit Polyclonal - **Target Antigen:** STAT3 - **Price:** $308.00 USD - **Format:** 100μl - **Applications:** WB, ELISA, IHC_P, ICC - **Species Reactivity:** Human, Mouse, Rat - **Formulation:** The antibody was affinity-purified from rabbit antiserum by affinity-chromatography using epitope-specific immunogen. Liquid in PBS containing 50% glycerol and 0.03% sodium azide. - **Shipping:** This product will ship in a box containing blue ice at a temperature of 4°C. - **Storage:** 4°C; -20°C for long term storage - **Description:** The protein encoded by this gene is a member of the STAT protein family. In response to cytokines and growth factors, STAT family members are phosphorylated by the receptor associated kinases, and then form homo- or heterodimers that translocate to the cell nucleus where they act as transcription activators. This protein is activated through phosphorylation in response to various cytokines and growth factors including IFNs, EGF, IL5, IL6, HGF, LIF and BMP2. This protein mediates the expression of a variety of genes in response to cell stimuli, and thus plays a key role in many cellular processes such as cell growth and apoptosis. The small GTPase Rac1 has been shown to bind and regulate the activity of this protein. PIAS3 protein is a specific inhibitor of this protein. Three alternatively spliced transcript variants encoding distinct isoforms have been described. - **Host:** Rabbit - **Isotype:** IgG - **Molecular Weight:** 88kDa - **Gene ID:** 6774 - **Dilutions:** - WB: 1/500 - 1/2000 - ELISA: 1/10000 - IHC: 1/200 - 1/1000 - ICC: 1/200 - 1/1000 - **Immunogen:** Synthesized non-phosphopeptide derived from human STAT3 around the phosphorylation site of serine 727. - **References:** 1. J Biol Chem. 2010 Jul 16;285(29):22414-25. 2. Int J Oncol. 2010 Jul;37(1):103-10. 3. Clin Immunol. 2010 Sep;136(3):442-6. Please note that the lead time for this product is not currently on file. - **Price:** $308.00 USD  
- **Format:** 100μl  
- **Applications:** WB, ELISA, IHC_P, ICC  
- **Species Reactivity:** Human, Mouse, Rat  
- **Formulation:** The antibody was affinity-purified from rabbit antiserum by affinity-chromatography using epitope-specific immunogen. Liquid in PBS containing 50% glycerol and 0.03% sodium azide.  
- **Shipping:** This product will ship in a box containing blue ice at a temperature of 4°C.  
- **Storage:** 4°C; -20°C for long term storage  
- **Description:** The protein encoded by this gene is a member of the STAT protein family. In response to cytokines and growth factors, STAT family members are phosphorylated by the receptor associated kinases, and then form homo- or heterodimers that translocate to the cell nucleus where they act as transcription activators. This protein is activated through phosphorylation in response to various cytokines and growth factors including IFNs, EGF, IL5, IL6, HGF, LIF and BMP2. This protein mediates the expression of a variety of genes in response to cell stimuli, and thus plays a key role in many cellular processes such as cell growth and apoptosis. The small GTPase Rac1 has been shown to bind and regulate the activity of this protein. PIAS3 protein is a specific inhibitor of this protein. Three alternatively spliced transcript variants encoding distinct isoforms have been described.
- **Host:** Rabbit  
- **Isotype:** IgG  
- **Molecular Weight:** 88kDa  
- **Gene ID:** 6774  
- **Dilutions:**  
  - WB: 1/500 - 1/2000  
  - ELISA: 1/10000  
  - IHC: 1/200 - 1/1000  
  - ICC: 1/200 - 1/1000  
- **Immunogen:** Synthesized non-phosphopeptide derived from human STAT3 around the phosphorylation site of serine 727.  
- **References:**  
  1. J Biol Chem. 2010 Jul 16;285(29):22414-25.  
  2. Int J Oncol. 2010 Jul;37(1):103-10.  
  3. Clin Immunol. 2010 Sep;136(3):442-6.

Please note that the lead time for this product is not currently on file. If you have any further questions or need assistance, feel free to ask!

Sales reply:
Dear Beny,

Generally antibody purification from hybridoma culture is more costly and takes more time than with ascitic fluid. This is because the hybridoma needs to be cultured in media containing FBS or some other sera. When the supernatant is purified by protein G, a lot of the resulting antibody will be from the sera supplement. To address this, we could (1) conjugate the antigen to a purification column or (2) adapt the hybridoma to serum-free media. Both methods would take some time and would cost at least an extra $1000-$2000, on top of the hybridoma culturing. The pricing breakdown can be found here .

For ascitic fluid collection, we will implant the hybridoma into the mice peritoneal space and collect the resulting ascites. This ascites will be purified by protein G and resuspended in PBS + sodium azide (though we can remove the preservative if you'd like)."}, {'submission_id': '1738955744262|bshapiro@evaluatediagnostics.com', 'reply_index': 11, 'reply_total': 33, 'submitted_at': '2025-02-07T19:15:44.262000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2025-04-03T16:37:22Z', 'sender_name': 'Beny Shapiro', 'institution': 'EVALUATE DIAGNOSTICS', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: Client Request - Antibody-DNA Conjugate', 'service_of_interest': 'Antibody Production', 'products_of_interest': 'Monoclonal Antibodies', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:

I do want to note that these antibodies are purified from murine ascitic fluid and not from tissue culture, which is why the turnaround time is a little longer.'}, {'submission_id': '1738955744262|bshapiro@evaluatediagnostics.com', 'reply_index': 33, 'reply_total': 33, 'submitted_at': '2025-02-07T19:15:44.262000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2025-05-21T22:49:12Z', 'sender_name': 'Beny Shapiro', 'institution': 'EVALUATE DIAGNOSTICS', 'reply_sender_name': 'Esmeralda Hernandez', 'reply_subject': 'Re: Client Request - Antibody-DNA Conjugate', 'service_of_interest': 'Antibody Production', 'products_of_interest': 'Monoclonal Antibodies', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [{'id': '190434904911', 'name': 'Cat#31423 31424 Lot 170925 Purified Antibody QC Report', 'extension': 'pdf', 'type': 'DOCUMENT', 'url': 'https://api-na2.hubspot.com/filemanager/api/v2/files/190434904911/signed-url-redirect?portalId=23756638', 'size': 146905, 'status': 'ok'}], 'page_content': 'Customer message:

Kind Regards

Beny Shapiro, PhD

Research Scientist

eValuate Diagnostics, Inc.

111 Ideation Way,

Nutley, New Jersey 07110

[cid:2b591c42-52c6-43a2-bee4-b25d96dd2d30]

Sales reply:
Hi Beny,

I hope this email finds you well. I apologize for the delay!

We run QC for each batch of antibodies before adding it to our inventory.

My understanding is that the extra bands with high molecular weights are some other forms of the antibody. Some antibodies form different forms (not always in a monomer state). We ran an SDS-PAGE of both antibodies side by side which showed two bands with H(~50kDa) and L (~25kDa) under the reduced condition. This shows that the purity is good and there is no need to worry about it. This antibody tends to have other forms under the non-reduced condition.

Attached is the PDF file of the gel that we reran, for this gel we used 1-2ug of antibody.'}]} [Re: 240910_MAB10525_Customer Consult_Abnvoa_30240] Customer message:
PO# 4500088488
Item Name:     PROM1 monoclonal antibody, clone 3F10
CatID#     30240
Lot# 170418

Our customer would like to know the concentration of the antibody for the corresponding batch. 
Unfortunately, we couldn't find this information on the Certificate of Analysis (COA). Could you please provide the details?

Thank you for your assistance.

---

Sales reply:
Hi Silvia,
Thank you for your inquiry.
30240 is formulated in ascitic fluid and the concentration is unknown since it is not purified.

[Catalog Antibody Product Question] Customer message:
I saw on your catalogue three primary antibodies for CD14, Cat No 20020, 30585, and 30590. Do you know if any of these combinations would work as an ELISA pair?

---

Sales reply:
Dear Shivali Kohli,

Thank you for your interest in our catalog antibody products!

We unfortunately have not tested our catalog antibody in pairs, so I am unsure of how these three antibodies would work together though they have all been tested to work well by themselves. My apologies for this and I hope it works well for you!

[Client Request - Antibody-DNA Conjugate] Customer message:
We interested in purchasing an antibody-DNA conjugate that we could use for our diagnostics assay.

---

Sales reply:
Hello Beny,

Thank you for reaching out and for your interest in ProMab!

We have over 25 years of experience in the antibody development industry and offer thousands of high-quality monoclonal antibodies. While we do not carry antibody-DNA conjugates as a regular product, we can certainly explore options to support your needs.

If you select an antibody from our catalog, we may be able to assist with the conjugation process either in-house using a commercial kit or through our trusted partners. Additionally, depending on the quantity purchased, we can offer a discount on the antibody and work with you on the conjugation.

Here is the link to our antibody products: https://www.promab.com/products/primary-antibody: https://www.promab.com/products/primary-antibody

Please let us know which antibody you are interested in and your specific project requirements, and we’d be happy to discuss potential solutions. If a direct conjugation service is your primary need, we can also recommend alternative providers specializing in this work.

Looking forward to your response!

Hello, I am interested in whether your product PM-CAR-1002 can be further customized to expressed a fluorescence protein.  I will be contacting you sometime today to follow up.  Thank you,  Giulio

---

Sales reply:
Dear Giulio​ Paciotti​,

Thank you for your message and inquiry, and I am happy to hear of your interest in our chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) ready-to-use products!

We have a product, PM-CAR1005, which may fit what you are looking for. It recognizes the same CD19-scFv sequence and has a FLAG and GFP tag, though the CSD domain is CD28 instead of 41BB,'}, {'submission_id': '1707230498804|gpaciotti@zahavbioscience.com', 'reply_index': 2, 'reply_total': 4, 'submitted_at': '2024-02-06T14:41:38.804000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-02-07T17:25:20Z', 'sender_name': 'Giulio Paciotti', 'institution': 'Zahav Bioscience, LLC', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: ProMab CAR-T Products', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'CAR-T Cells', 'has_customer_message': False, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Sales reply (no preceding customer message):

That's my error- I'm confirming that PM-CAR1002 has a 41BB CSD."}, {'submission_id': '1707230498804|gpaciotti@zahavbioscience.com', 'reply_index': 4, 'reply_total': 4, 'submitted_at': '2024-02-06T14:41:38.804000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-02-20T18:06:13Z', 'sender_name': 'Giulio Paciotti', 'institution': 'Zahav Bioscience, LLC', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: ProMab CAR-T Products', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'CAR-T Cells', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': "Customer message:
Hi JuYeon,

We are getting close to placing an order for the various CAR-T cells but would like clarification on the mock CAR-T control.

As you confirmed PM-CAR1002 is targeted with CD19, utilizes 41BB as the CSD and expresses GFP and FLAG for tracking purposes.

I’d like to confirm that you also have the mock transduced controls. The controls lack the CD19 targeting moiety but express 4-1BB and GFP/FLAG as their CSD and tracking molecules, respectively.

Please provide a catalog number for the mock controls.

If the control is not available, we will have to select another set of CD19 targeted cell lines and controls for our studies.

Thank you for your assistance.

---

Sales reply:
Hi Giulio,

PM-CAR1002 does not have GFP or FLAG tags. Alternatives for this are PM-CAR1005 (anti-CD19, CD28 CSD, FLAG and GFP tags) or PM-CAR1071 (anti-CD19, 41BB CSD, GFP tag). However, we don't have any mock CAR-Ts that have GFP or FLAG tags; these are instead detected thanks to a TF tag. Sales reply:
Dear Yueji Wang,

Thank you for your inquiry. We currently do not offer any products for clinical use, but we do offer two anti-CD19 CAR constructs with a GFP tag for research use only. These constructs utilize the FMC63 scFv domain which is currently clinically approved. I am unaware of any GFP available for clinical use, but we can provide the sequence to you if necessary.
1) PM-CAR1005: CD19scFv-FLAG-CD28-CD3z, >>> GFP
2) PM-CAR1071: CD19scFv-41BB-CD3z-T2A-GFP

1e9 cells $35,000

Please let me know how you want to proceed and I can provide you with a formal quotation. Thank you again for your inquiry Yueji and I look forward to your reply.
queries.final_message:
*📝 Draft reply* _(CSR: please review & edit before sending)_

Thank you for your inquiry regarding the differences between PM-CAR1000 and PM-CAR1042. 

Here are the key distinctions:

1. **Target Antigen**:
   - **PM-CAR1000**: Targets Mock antigen.
   - **PM-CAR1042**: Targets CD19, which is expressed by all B cells and B cell precursors.

2. **Description**:
   - **PM-CAR1000**: No specific description provided.
   - **PM-CAR1042**: Described as having a humanized CD19-specific scFv, making it suitable for targeting B cell malignancies.

3. **Construct**:
   - **PM-CAR1000**: Construct is mock-TM28-CD28-CD3ζ.
   - **PM-CAR1042**: Construct is CD19 scFv-TM-CD28-CD3z.

Both products are priced at $1259 USD and share the same formulation, shipping, and storage conditions. If you have any further questions or need additional information, feel free to ask!

ProMab's PM-CAR1042 primary human CAR-T cells express an anti-CD19 CAR containing a humanized CD19-specific scFv, hinge and transmembrane domains, the CD28 costimulatory domain and the CD3zeta activation domain. The CAR-T cells are generated by transducing activated human T cells with replication-defective lentivirus encoding the CAR. After expansion, the cells are cryopreserved and shipped in vials containing up to 10 million cells. Please inquire for orders over 10 million cells. All ProMab cells are guaranteed to be sterile, pathogen-free and mycoplasma-free.`

custom CAR-T development service | custom CAR-T development service | car_t query: tell me about your custom CAR-T development service. matches_found: 8. threads_returned: 3. threads: {'submission_id': '1711395226519|mark@immunofyx.com', 'best_score': 0.5707936063855507, 'reply_count': 6, 'units': [{'submission_id': '1711395226519|mark@immunofyx.com', 'reply_index': 1, 'reply_total': 6, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-25T19:33:46.519000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-03-25T20:00:00Z', 'sender_name': 'Mark Williams', 'institution': 'ImmunoFyx', 'reply_sender_name': 'Timothy Nguyen', 'reply_subject': 'ProMab Custom CAR-T / CAR-Treg Development', 'service_of_interest': 'Custom CAR-T Cell Development', 'products_of_interest': '', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Are you able to make CAR-Tregs ?

---

Sales reply:
Dear Mark Williams,

Thank you for your inquiry. We have spoken a few times over the past years regarding a bunch of different services. We are happy to help you generate CAR-T cells using T-reg’s. We have performed this for clients before.

Please see below our standard service flyer for custom CAR-T development.
Custom CAR-T Development.pdf: https://hubs.ly/Q02qDfZ50

Please note that the flyer is not entirely accurate, as it is representative of a project using normal PBMC, not T-regs. If we are working with T-regs, we will have to modify Phase IV of the service to charge additional fees to perform the T-reg isolation and expansion steps as they are a bit different than normal T cells. However, Phase III and Phase V will remain the same.

Happy to discuss this more over a conference call with you. Hope this preliminary information helps and I look forward to hearing back from you.'}, {'submission_id': '1711395226519|mark@immunofyx.com', 'reply_index': 2, 'reply_total': 6, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-25T19:33:46.519000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-03-29T18:04:11.332Z', 'sender_name': 'Mark Williams', 'institution': 'ImmunoFyx', 'reply_sender_name': 'Timothy Nguyen', 'reply_subject': 'Re: ProMab Custom CAR-T / CAR-Treg Development', 'service_of_interest': 'Custom CAR-T Cell Development', 'products_of_interest': '', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': "Customer message:
Dear Tim,

yes we have - I am glad you remember.

One quick question: how do you expand/propagate the cells after they have been transduced ? Or do you have to go back to fresh pool of primary cells ?

---

Sales reply:
Hello Mark,

Sorry for the delay in my response! For the T-reg's, sometimes we have noticed that a non-transduced or non-Treg population outgrows. Unless we are able to completely isolate a >99% population of T-regs, which is difficult to say the least, they typically do not keep the T-reg subpopulation over long periods of time. We can also ship them fresh to you to help amend any loss due to cryopreservation."}, {'submission_id': '1711395226519|mark@immunofyx.com', 'reply_index': 3, 'reply_total': 6, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-25T19:33:46.519000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-05-10T19:59:05Z', 'sender_name': 'Mark Williams', 'institution': 'ImmunoFyx', 'reply_sender_name': 'Timothy Nguyen', 'reply_subject': 'Re: ProMab Custom CAR-T / CAR-Treg Development', 'service_of_interest': 'Custom CAR-T Cell Development', 'products_of_interest': '', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Hi Timothy,

Could I get a quote to produce two vectors containing the ScFv and selected CAR domains ?

---

Sales reply:
Hi Mark,

content_blocks.body:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for your inquiry about our custom CAR-T development service. While I don't have specific details available at the moment, I can share that we offer a comprehensive service for developing CAR-T cells tailored to your project needs. 

For more detailed information, I recommend reviewing our service flyer, which outlines the general process, timelines, and pricing. Unfortunately, I cannot provide a direct link to the flyer at this time, but I can assist you further if you have specific questions or would like to discuss your project in more detail.

Please let me know how you would like to proceed, and I look forward to assisting you further.

Best regards,

[Your Name]  
ProMab Biotechnologies

queries.final_message:
*📝 Draft reply* _(CSR: please review & edit before sending)_

Thank you for your inquiry about mammalian protein expression. 

The process typically involves several key steps:
1. **Recombinant Vector Construction**: This begins with combining the PCR product with selected vectors to create a recombinant vector, which is then introduced into E. coli to generate recombinant plasmids.
2. **Plasmid Preparation**: After antibiotic selection and sequencing, a transfection-grade expression plasmid is prepared.
3. **Small-Scale Testing**: Initial testing is conducted in small-scale HEK293 cells to evaluate the expression.
4. **Larger-Scale Transfection**: Following successful small-scale tests, the workflow moves to larger-scale transient transfection in HEK293 or CHO cells for protein production.
5. **Purification and Evaluation**: Finally, the produced proteins are purified and analyzed using SDS-PAGE to assess the final expression and quality.

If you have any further questions or need more detailed information, feel free to ask!

Sales reply:
Dear Dongshan​ Yang​,

Thank you for your inquiry about our mRNA-LNP technology platform!

Here is our publication: https://www.mdpi.com/2072-6694/15/10/2860 where we have used our mRNA-LNPs to inject intratumorally in-vivo, and we have internally injected mRNA-LNPs IM for our studies. Our mRNA-LNPs are formulated ready-to-use and can be used in your in-vitro and in-vivo studies without further prep!

PM-LNP-0020 and -0020 have the same sequence but different promoters. We no longer offer PM-LNP-0020 and recommend PM-LNP-0021 for your research use.

Please let me know if you have any questions or would like a formal quotation. I will keep you updated with the estimated ship out date.'}, {'submission_id': '1710429596071|doyang@umich.edu', 'reply_index': 10, 'reply_total': 30, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-14T15:19:56.071000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-10-31T19:11:47Z', 'sender_name': 'Dongshan Yang', 'institution': 'University of MIchigan', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: mRNA-LNP Development Service', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'mRNA / LNP', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Hi, Ju Yeon,

We got some promising results using the LNP-EGFP. We are interested in testing Cas9 editing using this LNP. Is it possible to package Cas9 mRNA together with sgRNA in this LNP?

---

Sales reply:
Hi Dongshan,

I am very glad to hear that the eGFP-LNP worked well!

Yes, it is possible to do Cas9 editing with the LNP. You can either:

1.

Transfect your cells with Cas9-mRNA-LNP, then electroporate them with sgRNA the next day

2.

Co-encapsulate Cas9-mRNA and sgRNA into the same LNP

There are pros and cons to each method. Are you trying to knock in or knock out a gene? What gene is it and in which cell line?'}, {'submission_id': '1710429596071|doyang@umich.edu', 'reply_index': 11, 'reply_total': 30, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-14T15:19:56.071000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-10-31T21:00:53Z', 'sender_name': 'Dongshan Yang', 'institution': 'University of MIchigan', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: mRNA-LNP Development Service', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'mRNA / LNP', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Hi, Ju Yeon,

I am interested in in vivo delivery and editing in retina. It will be great to package Cas9 mRNA (100ug/ml ) and sgRNA(10 ug/ml) into the same LNP. Is there an option to order the empty LNP and add our customized mRNA/gRNA before transfection (something like the lipofectamine)? Or I can send you the mRNA or the template plasmid and synthesized sgRNA for you to package into the LNP? What will be the cost?

---

Sales reply:
Hi Dongshan,

Oh, that is very interesting - to my understanding, eye injections are very limited in volume, is that correct? What is the volume that you are currently working with?

To elaborate a little on the mRNA-LNP generation process, we essentially "feed" a mix of the mRNA as well as the lipid mix into the PreciGenome Flex machine. There's always a little bit of loss of RNA from this process as not all of the RNA that is fed in makes it in to the LNP. This is called "encapsulation efficiency", which is currently at an average of 70%.

I explain this to provide some background as to why the first method (ordering empty LNP and then adding the mRNA/gRNA) wouldn't work. But The second method you proposed will work perfectly. We can just package the mRNA and sgRNA at a 10:1 ratio so that the resulting RNA-LNP will theoretically end up at these concentrations.

I do want to note that as this would be a custom project, the starting cost will be a little higher. Can you let me know how much of the mRNA and sgRNA you will provide?'}, {'submission_id': '1710429596071|doyang@umich.edu', 'reply_index': 12, 'reply_total': 30, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-14T15:19:56.071000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-11-01T01:08:19Z', 'sender_name': 'Dongshan Yang', 'institution': 'University of MIchigan', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: mRNA-LNP Development Service', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'mRNA / LNP', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Thank you for your explain. We typically inject 50 ul/eye. I am thinking of starting at 20 ug mRNA/gRNA in 200 ul LNP first to test. If it really works, we will use large scale production for drug development.

---

Sales reply:
Hi Dongshan,

The machine that we would be using for this scale (the Flex(s)) has a maximum volume load, so it may be possible that the yield you need may not fit with the volume requirements. In order to calculate some numbers on my end, can you provide me more details with how much of the sgRNA and the Cas9-mRNA will you be providing (concentration and volume), and at what ratio do you want these encapsulated?'}, {'submission_id': '1710429596071|doyang@umich.edu', 'reply_index': 13, 'reply_total': 30, 'submitted_at': '2024-03-14T15:19:56.071000Z', 'reply_timestamp': '2024-11-01T21:27:39Z', 'sender_name': 'Dongshan Yang', 'institution': 'University of MIchigan', 'reply_sender_name': 'Ju Yeon  Lee', 'reply_subject': 'Re: mRNA-LNP Development Service', 'service_of_interest': '', 'products_of_interest': 'mRNA / LNP', 'has_customer_message': True, 'attachments': [{'id': '182268951632', 'name': 'Estimate 114024', 'extension': 'pdf', 'type': 'DOCUMENT', 'url': 'https://api-na2.hubspot.com/filemanager/api/v2/files/182268951632/signed-url-redirect?portalId=23756638', 'size': 59250, 'status': 'ok'}], 'page_content': 'Customer message:
Hi, Ju Yeon,

For testing, I will only need the same amount as the GFP LNP we used (20 ug mRNA+ 2 ug sgRNA in 200 ul LNP). I will provide mRNA and sgRNA for you to package into the LNP. Please send me a quote for this. We can talk about the large scale production if it works.

---
